thinks.sub: Every project passes through three legal perspectives. Consultation principle — no gap left open.

a.q: Can the project be realised at this location at all?
a.b: BauGB §§ 30 / 34 / 35, BauNVO zone types, B-plans and F-plans. The output: is building permitted here — and under what conditions.

b.q: Which permit procedure is required, and what does the building code demand?
b.b: BayBO with building classes, special-use cases and three procedure paths. Fire safety, parking, accessibility, GEG.

c.q: Which project-relevant requirements exist beyond planning and building law?
c.b: Encumbrances, heritage protection, municipal ordinances, use-specific permits — the area where projects often actually stall.

cmp.sub: What stands today between idea and application — and what it could be.

after.label: WITH PLANNING MATRIX
after.s2: Planning Matrix walks you through procedure & documents

trust.sub: Provenance, auditability, clear responsibility. No "the AI said so".

t1.label: AUDIT LOG · LIVE
t1.p: Every state change is timestamped, actor-stamped, immutable.

t2.label: PROVENANCE
t2.p: LEGAL · CLIENT · DESIGNER · AUTHORITY — combined with CALCULATED · ASSUMED · DECIDED · VERIFIED. You always know where a value comes from.

t3.label: ADMIN-NATIVE
t3.p: Vocabulary, procedure paths and document lists matching German administrative reality.

pricing.sub: Per seat at the table. Save annually, cancel monthly.

items.q: Does Planning Matrix replace my architect?
items.a: No. Planning Matrix prepares decisions and makes them transparent. The formal sign-off — and the legal responsibility — remain with the certified architect. We are the tool at the table, not the person who signs.
Bavaria is live. North Rhine-Westphalia and Baden-Württemberg are rolling out. Further states follow step by step — we maintain each LBO with care, not superficially.

items.q: How current is the legal data?
items.a: The central sources (BauGB, BayBO, BauNVO) are updated within 14 days of each amendment. Municipal ordinances are pulled in per address. Every statement leads to its source.

items.q: Can I use Planning Matrix before acquiring a plot?
items.a: Yes. Without a plot the system uses standard assumptions and marks affected areas accordingly — you get a realistic picture of the bandwidth, not a pseudo-precise number.
Every data change in a project — fact creation, qualifier change, recommendation update — is recorded immutably with timestamp, actor and reason. You can always show how a decision came to be.
Frankfurt am Main, in the EU. GDPR-compliant. No third-party sharing. Projects are deleted on request — including all backups within 30 days.

cta.sub: Four minutes to your first reliable assessment. Free of charge, without onboarding your architect.

footer.updatesSub: About once a month. What changes legally, in product, and in rollout. No marketing.

actions.newProjectSub: Two questions and you are at work

q1.sub: Pick the option closest to your project.
q1.h: What would you like to build?

help.link: Unsure? Open a short decision guide
help.body:
Are you building on an empty plot? Then **Single-family home** (for one family) or **Multi-family home** (multiple units).

Does a building already exist? Modernise it → **Renovation**. Change its use → **Change of use**. Add a storey → **Storey addition**. Extend it → **Side extension**.

Should a building be removed? → **Demolition**. Something that doesn’t fit here? → **Something else**, we begin with the most flexible assumptions.

standup.title: Stand up & look around
standup.confirmPlanning: Confirm planning law: is there a qualified Bebauungsplan?
standup.resumePrompt: Take a breath. Sit back down whenever you're ready.

30.head: BauGB · Section 30
30.title: Admissibility within qualified development plans
30.summary: Projects are admissible when they do not contradict the provisions of a qualified development plan and access is assured. Section 30 is the strictest of the three BauGB admissibility regimes — plan provisions are binding.

34.head: BauGB · Section 34
34.title: Admissibility inside built-up areas (Innenbereich)
34.summary: Inside built-up areas without a development plan, admissibility follows the character of the immediate surroundings. Section 34 requires the project to fit the type and scale of existing land use — even without a plan.

35.head: BauGB · Section 35
35.title: Building in the outer area (Außenbereich)
35.summary: Projects in the outer area are generally inadmissible unless privileged (subsection 1) or otherwise admissible (subsection 2). The bar is high — most residential projects do not qualify for privileged status.

2.head: BayBO · Article 2
2.title: Definitions — building classes 1–5
2.summary: Defines building classes 1–5 by height (7 m / 13 m / 22 m), usage units, and floor area. The class determines the procedure type and the requirements for fire protection, structural integrity, and submission authority.

57.head: BayBO · Article 57
57.title: Approval exemption (Genehmigungsfreistellung)
57.summary: Residential buildings in classes 1–3 within a qualified development plan are exempt from approval under tight conditions. The municipality has a 1-month review window; special buildings and structural alterations are excluded.

58.head: BayBO · Article 58
58.title: Simplified building permit procedure
58.summary: The most common procedure type for residential buildings in classes 1–3 when Article 57 does not apply. Only planning law and local building regulations are reviewed — structural integrity, fire protection, and energy compliance remain the owner's responsibility.

59.head: BayBO · Article 59
59.title: Regular building permit procedure
59.summary: Full review of all requirements — planning law, building regulations, and other public-law provisions. Used for larger projects outside the simplified procedure; statutory deadline is 3 months.

6.head: BayDSchG · Article 6
6.title: Heritage permission for alterations to listed buildings
6.summary: Alterations to or near listed buildings require a heritage permit. The permit is required in addition to the building permit — without it, even an otherwise exempt project is inadmissible.

3.head: BaumschutzV · Section 3
3.title: Protected trees — trunk circumference ≥ 60 cm
3.summary: In Munich, trees with a trunk circumference of 60 cm or more (measured at 1 m height) are protected. Felling, heavy interventions, or root damage require permission — including in private gardens.
